rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts inside annex agree {** ... **}; blocks within the appropriate component scope.
Use assume/guarantee/eq/const in component types for interface behavior; use assign in implementations for bindings.
generic_rules: Refer to component ports only through valid variables or outputs in type contracts.
Use visible identifiers and typed features to define assumptions and guarantees.
Encode concrete bounds or relations in assumptions and guarantees using named constants or expressions.
Avoid referencing AADL properties like Compute_Execution_Time or unit tokens directly in AGREE expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax includes assume "label": expression; guarantee "label": expression; eq name : type = expression; const name : type = value; assign output = expression;.
Component type annexes are for structural assumptions and guarantees; implementation annexes are for bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level assignments in component type scopes.
Do not reference AADL timing properties as AGREE variables without explicit typed ports or constants.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and their scope in AGREE
Model fusion and architecture redesign during repair
